tribe_description:
This tribe evaluates products like field testers for ordinary life: the item earns trust only if it performs its primary job on the first realistic attempt in the exact scenario they bought it for (a rushed morning, a quick touch-up, in the car, at a desk, on a trip). Their core belief is that “baseline success” should not require the customer to invent technique, add vigilance, or perform ongoing manual compensation. If it needs multiple passes, awkward hand repositioning, or workaround pressure just to do what it claims, they treat that as a design failure—and the review typically opens with the single observable symptom that proves the mismatch.

They practice a disciplined, non-theatrical form of evidence. Instead of building a long case with speculative downstream problems, they choose one decisive constraint (a geometry that can’t reach, a mechanism that jams, a surface that won’t conform) and treat it as sufficient to explain why the product won’t map onto real routines. Even when something is “fine in the spots it can reach,” they won’t inflate that into broad capability; they keep “limited-but-okay” separate from “works as advertised.” Just as importantly, they refuse to downgrade performance based on mere adjustment costs. If the form factor is different (shorter, smaller, unusual grip), they’ll label it as “getting used to it” unless it demonstrably reduces results.

Risk changes their proof style. For skin/hair-contact devices and powered tools, they treat the absence of harm events as affirmative evidence and will explicitly certify what did not happen (no tugging, no cuts, no irritation). They don’t automatically add technique warnings to sound responsible; if it was safe in normal use, that safety is part of the performance claim. But when a credible hazard happens even once—especially an entrapment or jam that takes meaningful time to undo—their stance flips from evaluation to consumer-protection. They won’t bargain with “be careful” workarounds; the review becomes a veto anchored to how easily the hazard occurs in normal use and how hard it is to recover.

With multi-mode or multi-head products, they assume calibration is real work, and they describe that learning curve honestly. They will admit icons aren’t self-explanatory and endorse trial-and-error when that is the only truthful path to usability—but they also believe the product must teach safe first use without guesswork. When they do recommend something, the praise comes packaged as a repeatable protocol (what to use first vs what finishes, placement, settings boundaries by body area) and is validated in real contexts like driving or working. Secondary features are scored separately: they name the exact ownership tax (profiles, where data is stored, what you must redo), because their end goal is to make another buyer’s regret predictable and to define the product’s correct role (travel/backup vs home primary) based on its actual use architecture, not generic category rubrics.
tribe_definition: A tribe of hands-on value seekers who look for products that deliver on what they care about most. They generally prioritize hands-on use, value for money.
population_definition: Health-conscious consumers who evaluate supplements, personal care, and wellness products.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When evaluating a product, they anchor the verdict on whether the primary job works on the first practical attempt, not on a general impression of quality. They treat needing extra passes, repeated effort, or workaround technique to reach “normal” results as functional underperformance, and they open the review with the specific symptom of that failure. If the core benefit is genuinely strong, they will accept a small adjustment cost in form factor or handling—but they keep “getting used to it” separate from performance criticism unless it measurably reduces the outcome.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 11–12 gaps repeatedly show that the tribe keeps adjustment costs (e.g., different plate/format) distinct from capability and instead anchors on first-use outcome; predictions wrongly converted adjustment into invented limits/caveats and added pass-count adequacy rules even when results were strong (behavioral_gap; HIGH across multiple items).

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When a tool doesn’t match their body mechanics or routine, they diagnose one concrete geometry/fit constraint and treat it as decisive. They describe exactly what motion, reach, contact, or angle the design prevents and the specific manual compensation it would require to get partial results. They may add a minimal “works where it reaches” note, but they avoid turning a localized mismatch into unobserved global claims about discomfort or broader failure.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 11 gaps show the tribe stating a single mechanical mismatch plus the exact compensation required (e.g., pulling handles to create pressure) while still allowing a narrow ‘okay where it reaches’ carve-out; predictions over-expanded into untested comfort and outcome claims (missing_nuance; HIGH).

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When reviewing comfort- and safety-adjacent products that contact skin/hair, they use a pass/fail ‘no harm event’ standard as primary evidence. They explicitly certify what did not happen (no tugging, nicks, abrasion, irritation) as part of the performance proof, rather than adding hypothetical technique warnings to sound cautious. If a credible hazard occurs even once—especially entrapment or a jam that takes meaningful time to undo—they issue a consumer-protection veto and do not offer ‘usable if you’re careful’ hedges.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 11–12 gaps show predictions injecting conditional safety guidance and softening hazards, while actual reviews used explicit “no harm events” as affirmative proof and treated a single hazard with long resolution time as a permanent veto (HIGH, repeated).

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When a product requires setup or safe loading (attachments, heads, hair/skin loading paths), they treat unclear affordances as a core product defect, not a documentation nit. They expect the product to teach safe first use well enough that a normal buyer doesn’t have to guess at load direction, placement, or safe sequencing. If confusion plausibly causes harm or a jam, they document that gap as design responsibility and downgrade trust sharply.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 11 hazard gaps show the tribe centering “no instructions / had to guess” as the root failure and tying it to the severity of the incident; predictions reframed it as conditional convenience or a manageable technique issue (HIGH).

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When a product has multiple modes, heads, or attachments, they treat it as a self-guided calibration problem and narrate the learning curve honestly. They will state when icons or controls aren’t self-explanatory and may explicitly recommend trial-and-error as the realistic path to mastery. Their ‘it works’ claim is inseparable from a replicable sequence (what goes first vs what finishes) and disciplined setting/placement boundaries, not from generalized claims they didn’t test.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 11–12 gaps show the tribe framing experimentation as central (icons not clear; trial-and-error endorsed) and validating success through a specific head/sequence protocol; predictions assumed straightforward setup and over-generalized outcomes (HIGH).

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When validating wellness or relief devices, they require operational proof: context + placement + time window. They report exactly where they positioned it, how quickly the effect appeared, and whether the benefit holds while doing real activities (like driving or working) that stress the claim. They avoid substituting generic ‘feels smooth/comfortable’ language for these metrics when the product promise is functional relief.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 11 gaps show the tribe using explicit placement mapping and time-to-improvement (including endurance while driving/working), while predictions replaced this with generic comfort talk and omitted protocol metrics (missing_nuance; MEDIUM-HIGH).

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When judging devices with secondary features (navigation, multi-user handling, data storage, organization), they score those separately from the core function and call out the exact friction point as an ownership tax. They name what breaks in daily life—where readings are stored, how users/profiles work, what must be redone—rather than advising readers to ignore features. A strong core result earns credit, but it doesn’t erase recurring workflow friction that will reliably cost time.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 12 gaps show predictions assuming they’ll bypass confusing features, while the tribe instead identifies the specific data/profile architecture problem as the real burden (behavioral_gap; HIGH).

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When assessing portability, they define it as ‘usable via its real carry/use architecture,’ not just compact enough to pack. They test whether the mechanism actually enables the travel scenario (how it draws, stores, dispenses, charges, or operates in constrained spaces) and then assign a role boundary (travel/backup vs home primary) based on maneuverability and daily handling. They reject generic portability rubrics that ignore how the product must be operated on the go.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 12 gaps show the tribe grounding portability in a unique travel-use mechanism and using that architecture to set role-fit boundaries; predictions reduced portability to packing/toiletry logistics and generic performance (MEDIUM-HIGH).

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When a product claims durability or waterproof capability, they respond with bounded trust and write the boundaries as part of the ownership truth. They may accept that certain parts can get wet while keeping other parts intentionally dry, and they describe the handling protocol (what they expose, what they protect, how they dry/store immediately after). Their recommendation often includes these constraints because ‘rated for it’ is not the same as ‘how I’m willing to use it.’
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 12 gaps show predictions softening into generic wet/dry comfort, while the tribe used a stricter separation rule (e.g., head can get wet; body should stay dry) plus immediate drying protocol as their real-use standard (MEDIUM-HIGH).

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When a product disappoints (or succeeds), they resist review-padding and avoid inventing extra zones, use cases, or optimization thresholds they didn’t observe. They won’t add detailed area-by-area performance maps, pass counts, or special-user scenarios just to make the verdict sound more complete. In their logic, a clean report that another buyer can quickly map to their own routine is more trustworthy than speculative coverage.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 11–12 gaps repeatedly show predictions inventing ergonomics/body-area coverage, mouth-zone specifics, braces scenarios, or pass-count thresholds, while the tribe stayed disciplined to what they actually tested and observed (behavioral_gap; MEDIUM-HIGH).

main.text: When deciding what’s ‘worth it,’ they are motivated by a fairness equation: the price should match the real-world outcome and the buyer effort required to get that outcome. They dislike paying extra in time, discomfort, or workaround behavior to reach baseline performance, and they treat ‘almost works if you adapt’ as the seller shifting labor onto the customer. This is why they can accept a form-factor adjustment when it buys a real benefit, but they reject products that require ongoing compensation just to perform normally.
main.rationalization: Batch 11 gaps show the tribe treating form-factor adaptation as a tolerable adjustment rather than a capability downgrade, while still condemning repeated-pass baseline failure as design underperformance (HIGH across the window).

main.text: When a product touches the body or can fail dangerously, they are motivated by harm avoidance more than novelty or feature count. They want designs that prevent predictable failure modes without requiring constant vigilance, and they interpret unclear safe-use teaching as part of that risk. This drive explains why prolonged entrapment incidents become a non-negotiable veto and why they resist ‘just be careful / use the right technique’ rationalizations.
main.rationalization: Batch 11–12 gaps show the tribe treating missing safe-use teaching plus a single credible hazard as disqualifying and rejecting technique-caveat salvaging that predictions attempted (HIGH, repeated).

main.text: When evaluating relief or wellness devices, they are motivated by repeatable benefits that survive real contexts and longer sessions, not one-off impressions. They prefer evidence they can operationalize—placement, time-to-relief, and whether it works while driving or working—and they trust results more when the proof variables are stated plainly. They avoid inflating the story into medical identity claims when functional, context-tested evidence is sufficient.
main.rationalization: Batch 11 gaps emphasize context + placement + time-window as the tribe’s credibility baseline versus predictions’ generic comfort framing (MEDIUM-HIGH).

main.text: When shopping, they are motivated to avoid being burden-shifted by incomplete kits, missing organization, or suspicious ‘extras.’ They expect the package to include the practical essentials for day-one use and day-to-day storage, and they read mismatched attachments as a signal of sloppy kit design or marketing confusion. This reflects a belief that the seller should carry integration work, not the buyer improvising power sources, storage, or interpreting random add-ons.
main.rationalization: Batch 12 gaps show the tribe treating mismatched accessories as a kit-integrity signal (suspicion/marketing confusion) rather than a harmless bonus that can be ignored (HIGH).

implicit_goals.text: When they post a review, they aim to translate the product into a go/no-go decision for a specific routine and setting. They want readers to know whether it fits travel vs home, quick touch-ups vs longer sessions, and ‘out doing life’ constraints like fast access, maneuvering, and use in the car or at a desk. They often recommend by role (backup/travel vs primary/home) instead of offering a one-size-fits-all endorsement.
implicit_goals.rationalization: Batch 12 portability gaps reinforce that they decide by real use-architecture and role boundary rather than generic category scoring (HIGH).

implicit_goals.text: When warning other shoppers, they aim to surface the one reality that will cause regret—especially a hazard, missing safe-use teaching, or an unfixable geometry mismatch. They write to prevent buyers from reframing a design fault as user error, so they foreground veto reasons early and keep them concrete. If a credible hazard occurred and took time to undo, their goal shifts from ‘help you use it’ to ‘help you avoid it.’
implicit_goals.rationalization: Batch 11–12 hazard gaps show the tribe centering consumer-protection logic (single event + long resolution time) rather than offering conditional usability hedges (HIGH).

implicit_goals.text: When they like a product, they aim to leave behind a usable method that makes success repeatable without guesswork. They provide the exact sequence, placement, and boundaries that produced the result, and they will include a risk-management protocol when claims like waterproofing require bounded trust. The goal is that the next buyer can replicate the win without inventing technique or discovering safety constraints the hard way.
implicit_goals.rationalization: Batch 11–12 gaps show that their positive proof is a replicable protocol (sequence/placement) plus explicit handling boundaries, while predictions drifted into generic comfort praise or softened rules (HIGH).

AGCQR2QLP3XWU64TBYFJCAZWQQKQ.review_text: This product is actually really cool and just as easy to use in real life as it seems like in the pictures. It was really the easy to do on someone else but takes a little bit more time to do on yourself though if you get some on your skin or ears or wherever you can just take a wet damp towel or a white or something like that and wipe it right off. In the picture that shows you doing it too long hair but I tried it on my long hair and absolutely did not work I could not pull a comb through it and it was hard and strange and it definitely works better on men's hair that is short. When it was dry it got kind of hard like a gel or something like that. It's really easy to make it look nice you just have to continue working it through the hair until you get the desired color and style that you want. We tried it on a beard as well and it didn't look as good on the beard it kind of looked better on the medium to short hair but not super super short like a beard. It was really easy to wash out renting it out just with water gets a lot of it off but a little bit of shampoo get it out in just a second. I don't recommend going to bed with this in your hair as it will leave silver glimmery stuff on your pillowcase to make sure you wash before going to sleep or laying down on anything.

AEGKJTQWHGBIOTO34OB3GKIGCN7A.review_text: The Covid answer to post gym workouts!  If I were a gym owner,  I would keep these well in stock! This is a nice big cloth, used to clean up after a workout. I don't like that it's stated as a massager and it's not. Misleading
I do like the red light therapy with the heat that this device has. I know red light therapy is great to help with circulation. Have been using red light therapy for a few years now. So, I like having a device specifically for my neck area.
Does heat up nice and it's soothing.
But, if you're looking for a massager, this isn't it.

AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.product_description: FIDAC Tens Unit Muscle Stimulator, Best Rechargeable Dual Channel Tens Unit Machine, EMS Muscle Stimulator for Pain Relief Therapy with 24 Modes, 12 Electrode Pads (Black)
AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.review_text:
I like that I can wear this tens unit even when I'm out shopping. The main machine fits in my pocket easily.
Setting it up is easy. Just need to take the time to find the best mode and intensity to suit your needs.
Some areas I can do stronger intensity while other areas a lighter tough is needed.
This has so many modes to choose from. I, personally, love the deep tissue. Works great and feels so good on my back when I pull a certain muscle.
Gives enough pads to last awhile.
ONe of the better tens unit I've tried.

AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.product_description: USUIE Red Light Therapy Belt, Infrared Light
AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.review_text:
Wish they had something like this years ago when our dog had spine problems. Spent a lot of money taking her in for red light therapy sessions back then.
I saw this therapy belt and had to get it. I know what red light therapy does and how much it helps the body repair itself or increase circulation.
This therapy belt is so easy to use. Comes with velcro strips so I can have it on my belly, around my shoulder, or on my back. Longer velcro strip for larger areas. Smaller strip for going around elbows, knees or ankles.
I like that it has an automatic 20 minute shut down timer. This way I know I'm getting the maximum benefit from this therapy belt but not over doing it. It is comfortable to have on so it's easy to forget I do have it on and running.
I'm doing this twice a day as recommended. I want to see if this will help with my lower back pain. I know it'll take some time for me to notice any changes in my pain level.
This is so great to have! And to be able to use it on my dogs too. What a bonus that is.
I've seen, first hand, how much red light therapy can help both humans and dogs. And I'm excited to keep trying this and to always have it on hand to use after surgeries too for faster healing.

AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.product_description: EasyinBeauty Cordless Hair Straightener, Magnetic Charging Cordless Flat Iron, Portable Wireless Hair Straightener with 8000mAh Battery, Fast Heating Floating Plate with Ceramic Coating No Hair Damage
AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.review_text:
The first thing I love about this flat iron is that it's cordless! It's so nice to slowly getting rid of all of the power cords in my bathrooms.
The iron itself is nice. Heats up fast.
The only thing I don't like is the size of the heating plate. I'm used to a longer one in my older flat iron so it's taking me a bit to get used to this one.
Since I only straighten my bangs and every so often my entire head of hair, the charge will last me a long time before I need to recharge it again.
Nice flat iron that's easy to pack up and take on the go.

AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.product_description: Daily Roll-On for Instant Relief - Made from Organic Hemp Seeds Oil - Great Cooling Gel for Pure Body Relaxation by Alter Native - Made in The USA - 3 oz
AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.review_text:
Rarely do I rate a pain cream, spray, or roll on, high. Some may help the pain a bit but usually not for long.
I will say that this hemp roll on is good. Yes, it does have a strong menthol scent but it's not overwhelming unless you roll half the bottle on your body.
First thing I tried it on is the pain on the top of my feet. Almost instantly the pain was gone. I mean gone!
Nothing has been able to take my arthritis pain away for as long as this roll on did.
Next place I tried it on is my lower back. I've been hurting so bad for the last 4 days now so I was willing to try anything. I am sitting here right now with barely an ache in my lower back. I rolled it on my lower back about 20 minutes ago. Within 5 minutes I could feel relief. How long will the relief last? Not sure. It depends on how much activity I do today I'm sure.
It's not that expensive. Can't guarantee that it will take away your pain completely but, I'm betting it will at least take that big edge of pain off.
I'll be ordering more bottles of this for sure.

AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.product_description: XUYU Far Infrared Heating Pad Mat for Back, Neck, Shoulders, Cramps, Pain Relief with Natural Jade and Tourmaline, Auto Shut Off, Smart Timer and Heat Settings, 16” x 21
AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.review_text:
First thing I love is that it gets hotter than my other heating pads. And then I love that it's stiffer so I'm able to brace it up when I'm sitting in a chair.
Not exactly sure how the jade stone work but I like them. They press into my back and deliver more heat into my sore muscles.
Having the time and temperatures illuminated makes it easier for me to see at night when I'm having trouble sleeping and my joints are aching.
Have never had an infrared heating pad before and I am really liking this one.
Saves my settings. Has auto shut off in case I forget I have it on.
I tossed my other heating pad and am now using this one every single day.

AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.product_description: Sound White Noise Machine with 36 Soothing Sounds and Night Lights Sound Machines with Memory Function 36 Levels of Volume and 60 Sleep Timer for Sleeping Relaxation,Night Lights Sound Machines
AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.review_text:
I love how many different sounds and intensities there are.
I personally love the rain. I just love the sound of rain. It's soothing and really helps me relax to fall asleep.
So easy to use. I do like that it charges up using a USB cable.
On the nights when it's just too chilly to turn on my fan, I like that I can still have the sound of a fan running without the cold air blowing.
A great sound machine that can be used by anyone in the family.

AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.product_description: AVCOO 4 Channel TENS EMS Unit 24 Modes Muscle Stimulator for Pain Relief Therapy, Electronic Pulse Massager Machine with 12 Pads, Dust-Proof Bag, Fastening Cable Ties.
AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.review_text:
My old tens unit had just basic settings and intensities. Plus all pads had to be the same setting.
This tens unit is so different and so much better. First of all it's so much lighter. Easier to carry around so you can have it working even when I'm driving or working.
I can also use 8 pads at one time if I need to. I may hurt at times but the most pads I need are 4 to cover my sore areas.
I'm still learning what all the pictures on the device actually mean. The best way to learn is by just trying them all out to see what mode is best for you for a certain area and what intensity works the best.
My first encounter with a stims unit or called tens unit is at a chiropractors office. I would feel so good after going through a session.
After that I had to have one and purchased my first tens unit about 15 yrs ago. Haven't been without one since.
These are so good with helping with my tension headaches. I place the pads on my upper back/shoulder blade area. It really helps loosen the muscles and relieving my headaches.
They're also great with lower back pain. Pads placed on the lower back on each side of my spine and within about 90 mins I am feeling so much better.
Driving also makes my back hurt. I can have the pads on and running while I'm driving which really helps me sit for longer periods so I can drive longer distances.
For any muscle ache on any part of your body, minus the chest area, and the relief is priceless.
If you've never tried a tens unit, you really should try one out and see how good it feels after using one.
I like this unit. Comes with more pads than other units.

AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.product_description: MUNTO 12 Pack Replacement Toothbrush Heads Compatible with Philips Sonicare Electric Toothbrush with Click-on Handles, White
AELHYMCYVS6T35HA3UH5UGFWTK6Q.review_text:

Love finding good value packs so was happy to find these.

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: E-YOOSO Large Wireless Mouse, X-31 Large Mouse for Big Hands, 5-Level 4800 DPI, 6 Button Big Ergo Computer Mouse, 18 Months Battery Life Cordless Mouse for Laptop, Mac, Chromebook, PC, Windows(Black)

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: Kopari Bubblegum Lip Gloss Kit | Strawberry, Grape, and Bubble Gum Flavors | Hydrating Blend of Shea Butter and Coconut Oil to Nourish and Soothe Chapped Lips | Vegan and Cruelty-Free
AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.review_text: I love this trio of lipgloss. The flavors are very popular fragrances of strawberry, grape, and bubblegum. The strawberry and the grape do appear to be slightly tinted, but you will not notice any real color added to your lips from them. You can use them alone and enjoy just a nice clear gloss over your natural color lips, or you can put it over top of your color and no worry of changing the color of your favorite lipstick. It will add a very nice bit of shine and a little bit of a sparkle to whatever color you decide to slide it over.  I do not notice a lot of flavor to these glosses, but the color is very pretty. I feel that the box is a nice addition if you plan on giving these as a gift. I think this would make a wonderful gift for a young girl or even an older lady. I can’t imagine anybody that wouldn’t enjoy receiving this trio of lip glosses. I felt the price was extremely fair and that the quality of this item was very good.

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: ANGNYA 6Pcs Nail Art Brushes Set Gel Nail Polish Design Pen Painting Tools With Nail Extension Gel Brushs,Builder Gel Brush,Nail Art Liner Brush for Salon at Home DIY Manicure(Pink)
AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.review_text: This particular kit of nail art brushes had a good variety of different size brush heads.  I've got other kits and they tend to all either be really thin or larger.  This is a good kit to have because it's got a nice cross section of sizes to choose from.  I wanted a particular size to make snowflakes on my nails with snow dots and I can do both designs with this one kit.  I'm very happy with this section of brushes and the cost value too.

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: Peripera Ink Mood Matte Lipstick, Lightweight, Matte, Smooth, Hydrating, Lasting Color Payoff (02 Pink Go Up)
AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.review_text: I really love this color and the feel of this lipstick.  I was impressed too with how pretty the packaging was.  There's nothing nicer than when you take your lipstick out of your purse and everyone sees how pretty the tube is - but I digress.  This lipstick is a great nude color and goes well with just about anything I wear - be it red or pink.  You must always have a good versatile lipstick color in your purse at all times.  Your clothes change but you always take the same purse when you're out and about.  Your lipstick in that purse should go with everything you're wearing.  The feel of this lipstick is also very nice which is a huge plus.  I think the price is fair for such a nice quality lipstick.

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: FERYES Makeup Sponge Holder with Makeup Sponge– Eco-Friendly Silicone Beauty Make Up Blender Case and Dry & Wet Use Foundation Sponge for Travel - Khaki (MS Khaki)
AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.review_text: One issue I've had in my makeup drawer is that I hate to just put my blender sponge in there without being inside something to keep it clean and sanitary.  This solves that problem nicely.  It's very soft and pliable and opens with a squeeze of the ends of the container.  It's very easy to use and your sponge fits nicely inside.  It seems quite durable and like it would last a good long time.  It's also easy to clean and allows circulation in case you wash your sponge and it's slightly damp.  The air holes will allow for proper circulation.  This is a really handy gadget!

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: 2PCS Portable Electric Toothbrush Travel Case, Plastic Travel Toothbrush Container Holder Suitable for Oral-B, Philips Sonicare and Other Electric Toothbrush Set (Black and White)
AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.review_text: These toothbrush cases are a good size and you can fit your electric toothbrush inside easily if you remove the brush stem.  They won't rattle around in there because they have grooves where the brush and stem both can easily fit inside and clip into place.  The reason I gave these a 3 star rating was because the clip that is to keep the case closed doesn't really clamp or close right.  It's very loose and I think that you will need a rubber band to keep the case closed.  I know that's not really a big deal but given that I haven't even used these yet I can't blame wear and tear on the fact that the clasp doesn't work.  It didn't work from the moment I took it out of the box.  I suppose if you put this case inside a toiletry bag, it's unlikely it will open even without a rubber band so really, if you need something to pack up your electric toothbrush, these cases will work and hold most brands of electric toothbrush and the price was fair.

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: Little Moon Essentials Floral Bath & Shower Sugar Exfoliant, Relax, 2 oz.
AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.review_text: This has a very pleasant scent and works quite well at exfoliating and moisturizing your skin.  I think the scent is a bit on the stronger side but it's pleasant and is more like something you'd smell in a spa for relaxation.  It's a small size tin so use sparingly.  A little goes a long way.  You will enjoy this little luxury very much.  It's a great value as well as a fun thing to use or gift to a friend.

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: Cuticle & Nail Care Set 3pcs - Stainless Steel Cuticle Nippers, Cuticle Pusher And Scraper To Remove Gel Nail - Nail Art Remover Tool, Manicure Tools (Silver)
AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.review_text: I ordered this mainly for the cutters.  This cuticle nipper has a double tension resistance and is easy to maneuver.  I like to keep my nails and cuticles looking good.  It's important to have the right quality tools.  This kit has the basics and the price is right.  Each item seems to be of high quality steel and feels good in your hand making them an easy way to keep your manicure and pedicure looking good.

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: magicopper Face Masks with strap, Breathable Face Mask special 2 Layer Facemask, Lightweight Facial Masks for Adult, Men, Women, Indoor, Outdoor Use
AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.review_text: I wasn't sure what to expect when this arrive but I was pleasantly surprised at how breathable this mask is.  It's more formed and cone shaped on it's own which allows a breathing space between your face and the mask itself.  This makes this mask so easy to breath with.  I'm not used to using masks since I spend more time at home but when I do go out, I want a mask that doesn't make me feel like I'm struggling for breath.  This is the mask for you!  It not only is very safe and the harder shell surface will keep germs out, it's also a nice breathable mask.  The price is extremely fair as well.  It comes with a lanyard so you don't have to put your mask down on any dirty surface when not in use.  It can hang from your neck comfortably.  The inside of the mask is washable so you can use it again and again without fear.

AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.product_description: Calendula Soap With Coconut Oil Soap - Natural Body Soap | Best Body Bar For Dry & Sensitive Skin | Use As A Bath Soap & Face Pure Extracts (2.64 Ounce)
AEXO6YBJHQZFPLVRQJI6VX7IJDNA.review_text: It's got to be the Calendula oil that leaves your skin so moisturized after using this soap.  I first smelled the very subtle fragrance and thought I'd enjoy this soap enough to purchase it again till I also realized it's really moisturizing.  I think you'll be very happy with how it leaves your skin feeling most of all.  It's advertised as being all natural as well so if you have sensitive skin, you shouldn't have any problems with this although I'd always recommend trying a test patch first before using it in the shower all over.

I love that this is Vegan!  It's got good things for your skin other than the relaxing qualities of Lavender.  It's got Vitamin A and Almond Oil in this lotion.  It comes out very thick and creamy.  The scent is amazing too.  It is very calming for you and I'd suggest using it right before bed.  It's just the ticket to a good sleep regimen.  You will love this lotion as much as I do I'm certain of it!

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: Shiatsu Neck and Back Massager,Carbon Brush Disc Safety Thermostatic Back Massager with Heat,8 Light and Heavy Massage Nodes Back Massager for Back Pain,Pure Copper Motor Massagers for Neck and Back (Yellow2)
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text: My daughter has chronic tightness in her upper back and neck and constantly gets knots in her muscles in that area.  In addition to other massage techniques she uses, this pillow-type automated massager is an easy way to get a simple 15-minute massage session to help relax her muscles and free up those knots.  Both the speed and "direction" of the massage can be adjusted between 2 settings and the direction change provides a nice variety.  The heating is useful but not too hot that it becomes uncomfortable or dangerous (in terms of burning).  The construction materials seem to be of decent quality and the pillow itself is attractive.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: Mens Nose Hair Trimmer with Beard Trimmer Eyebrow Trimmer,Nose Hair Trimmer for Men Cordless Rechargeable,3-in-1 Nose & Ear Hair Trimmers for Facial Hair Body Hair Wet Dry Use Waterproof Shaver
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
I use this kit to trim my facial hair - nose hair, ear hair, side burns, and eyebrows. Work great!

WHAT I LIKE:
* Compact -- small enough to travel with if you need to. Takes up minimal space on vanity.
* Rechargable -- much better than battery operated. No need to replace batteries
* Easy to swap attachments -- just a small twist to remove and install
* All attachments do what they need to - eyebrows, side burns, nose hair, ear hair
* Small brush to clean out any hair still on the blades
* Can rinse tip under stream of water without concern of damage (would not submerge it though)

WHAT I DON'T LIKE:
* As typical these days, it does not come with a charging block -- just a USB charging cable
* No travel case or storage tray/case so there is no easy way to organize the attachments on the vanity

SUMMARY:
A nice compact facial hair trimmer at a reasonable price -- currently just under $20 with free Prime shipping.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: CITAITAI Water Flosser Cordless, Protable Water Flosser IPX7 Oral Irrigator for Teeth, Gums, Braces, Dental Care,with 4 Modes,5 Water Jet Tips for Multiple Users and Needs,USB Rechargeable-300ML
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
I use a water flossing system every night at home rather than flossing since it is easier for me and the dentist claims it is actually better for my gums. When I travel, I use floss since my water floss system is large and bulky and would never make sense to travel with. For this device, its strength is actually that you can travel with the handle and use use the tube to pull water from a cup or container. I am not a big fan of using this at home since it is rather bulky and more difficult to maneuver than my current system where I just hold the small nozzle and the pump is located with the resevoir (which is what makes it non-viable to travel with).

With this new system, I now have a viable travel option for a water flosser as well as a backup for my at-home system if it stops working.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: Experience a blast of full-body coolness at the touch of a button. Gold Bond No Mess Talc-Free Body Powder Spray, Classic Scent has a unique triple action formula for your whole body and targeted hot spots. It cools on contact, absorbs moisture, and controls odor*. The 360-degree valve allows you to spray at any angle, even upside down. Best of all, Gold Bond Powder Spray keeps hands clean. Use Gold Bond No Mess Spray right before a workout or right after showering to keep cool, dry and comfortable. Now talc-free and formulated without parabens and preservatives. *Absorbs odor-causing sweat
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text: Glad to see this powder based body spray is talc-free.  With all the harmful effects being attributed to talc, I am still a bit skittish on using a powder too close to my face.  So far I am using it on my feet and it works well so keep them cool and dry.  After turning the cap to unlock, the sprayer is still a bit inconsistent.  Sometimes you have to press it a few times to get it to spray.  When it does spray, it comes out in a controlled fashion that allows me to apply it accurately to my feet.  Once it is applied, it is cooling, drying, and helps with odor.  Wish the spray mechanism worked more consistently.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: CLR PRO Automotive Heavy Duty Radiator Flush and Cleaner, 24 Ounces (Pack of 6)
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
I have seen traditional CLR used effectively to unplug heater cores and save a bundle on a heater core replacement. Also effective on removing rust and corrosion from radiator and cooling system if you are having overheating issues on older, high mileage vehicles. This CLR PRO should be even more effective than the normal CLR and certainly a safer bet than various home-brew flush solutions that could damage your cooling system. I like the fact that it is non toxic and biodegradable, which is pretty surprising given the job that this has to do to break down calcium, lime, and rust (hence, CLR) and remove all that scale and clogging. But the description clearly states "Non Toxic Products: CLR PRO Automotive products are safer alternatives and contain no phosphates, ammonia or bleach; Non toxic and readily biodegradable" which is awesome. The small pint and a half containers are nicer than the large gallon jugs in my opinion since you don't end up with half used jugs around and these smaller containers are easier to store.

For DIYers looking for tips on how to deep flush a heater core with CLR, there are lots of videos online, but I recommend searching on youtube for "sound main auto CLR" for a great short video on a great DIY flush technique that is very effective and uses inexpensive parts rather than expensive professional equipment.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: DAVEMED Mini First Aid Kits,Small Waterproof Emergency Medical Survival Kit for Camping,Hiking,Travel,Backpacking,Vehicle,Outdoors & Sports（78 Pieces）
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
This is a well constructed first aid bag that is very small so it is a great option when you are tight for space. It has a belt loop strap on it so you can run your belt loop through it and also has a couple of small loops you can run a cord, hook, or caribiner through to hang it on the outside of your backpack.

In terms of contents, it is very oriented toward bandages of various types so it is really made for treating scrapes and cuts. It has scissors, tweezers, gloves, and antisceptic wipes as well. The most unusual element is a large number of safety pins. They may come in handy, but seem out of place compared to other parts of the kit. There is so much stuff in there that it is a tight fit to get it all back in the case.

I am keeping this in the trunk of my car for emergencies and can take it out with me on a spontaneous hike or climb as well. The compact size makes it a great option when you are really tight on space. Would work great on a bicycle.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: Noise Reduction Ear Plugs for Sleeping, 4 Pairs Ultra Soft Reusable Silicone Ear Plugs Noise Canceling Ear Plugs for Sleeping, Swimming, Snoring, Concerts
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
These ear plugs are soft and comfortable and easy to install. Once you get them in to where they seal, they do very well at lowing the sound level. Because they are very soft, they are pretty comfortable. I was a bit concerned about the "handle" on the blue ones that I thought my be a problem when sleeping on my side, but it was not an issue (at least for me) since the handle sits completely in my ear (kind of angled downward toward my earlobe). I like the individual cases for easy transport, storage, and reuse. I think these would work well on for travel (both on a plane as well as in a noisy hotel room). The one pair of black ear plugs work similarly well and are also very soft -- the handle on these is different, so they may work better (or worse) than the blue ones for some folks depending on their ear size and form. The one drawback with the black ones is that they don't have a plastic storage case like the blue ones.

It would be better if they came with some instructions and guidance, but I was able to figure out how to insert them and get them to seal. When dealing with your ears, it is always best to have guidance and instructions though.

Price of around $16 (currently) with free Prime delivery seems reasonable at $4 per pair.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: 3 Pairs Heel Cushion Pads Self-Adhesive Shoe Insoles Cushions Liners for Men and Women 3 Colors for All Shoes
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
This is a pretty nice set of heel cushions. I like the mesh material better than the leather material I have used in the past -- they are softer and more breathable and I think it makes them more comfortable (hopefully, they are as durable over time). The grey, black, and beige color options allow you to match your shoes so they don't stand out as much. These are generally useful if you get abrasion on the back of your heal and need extra protection/cushioning OR if your shoes/bookts are a bit large and your heal slips up every time you raise your foot.

The key with these is to place them properly and to get it "right on the first attempt". If you get them in there wrong and have to pull them off and retry, you will lose the adhesion and they will not stay on as well over time. I recommend testing out the placement without removing the backing to make sure you have them where you want them. Then remove the backing and fold in half, get the center aligned, and then push out the sides and press into place -- hopefully, you get it perfect on the first attempt.

My only concern with these is the lack of directions and lack of English description on the product. I recommend that the marketing company update the product packaging to include full English directions for the US market.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: Lady Shaver and Trimmer for Women-Electric Cordless Bikini Trimmer Electric Painless Lady Shavers Rechargeable Wet & Dry Hair Removal Epilator for Face and Body,Arms,Legs Blue…
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
This little cordless electric shaver works well on legs and underarms. The double edge razor works best for initial shave pass and then the foil head can be used for remaining short stubble as necessary. Both heads worked smoothly with no signs of nicks, abrasion, or irritation. The heads can be exposed to water and shave cream, but we prefer to keep the body of the shaver as dry as possible (despite the claimed IPX7 waterproof rating). If used in the shower, I would dry the body off quickly when I was done.

In terms of charging, it holds the charge plenty long for a shave and charges fully in under 2 hours. It uses a USB charging cable (included) -- and I wish it came with a wall charger, but no such luck (you have to provide your own USB power source).

The storage/carry bag is a convenient way to keep the kit together.

It is not clear what the guide combs are used for -- looks like beard grooming attachments used by men. They seem kind of useless for a women's razor (perhaps to loan to her husband if he forgot his razor on a trip?). Weird.

Overall, this is a nifty women's electric shaver that is effective and convenient.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: Skyfoot’s Silicone Gel Non-Slip Heel Cups Protector,Heel Cushion Insoles,for Plantar Fasciitis,Bone Spurs,Heel Pain Relief ,Shock Absorption, for Kids & Women & Men (1 Pair) (M)
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
These are a significant step up from some other gel heel protectors we have used in the past. My wife has a condition with her heel that can flare up from time to time and cause significant pain for weeks on end. When this happens, she really benefits from gel heel inserts to allow her to go about her business and still recover.

WHAT WE LIKE ABOUT THESE INSERTS:
* The deep cup with the gel coming up around the back of the heal for about 1/2 inch of back heel protection is really nice. Some others just provide the gel on the bottom of the heal. This one stabilizes the heal a bit with the cup and also provides some protection on the back and sides.
* A wave pattern on the inside where your heal rests provides extra grip and slip-resistance
* Around 1/4 inch of bottom silicone gel is about right to provide the protection and comfort she needs without creating excessive instability or raising the heel too much.
* The gel is sticky and stays in place in the shoe without need for adhesive (allowing you to easily move them from one pair of shoes to another)

AREA FOR IMPROVEMENT:
* We struggled a bit to pick the right size since there was not real guidance on how S, M, L matched to shoe sizes. There were dimensions on width, but that doesn't help most people. In the end, we got the M and it worked well for her size 8 feet. Would appreciate some extra guidance on the product page on sizing (referencing men and women shoe size ranges)

These are a bit more expensive than other options we have used -- but in our case, it is certainly worth it to get the extra features mentioned above. Particularly given how useful these are when my wife has a flare up.

SUMMARY:
A great set of silicone gel heel protectors for anyone suffering from heel pain.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: Ear and Nose Hair Trimmer Set - 2 in 1 Painless Facial Hair and Eyebrow Clipper for Men and Women Rechargeable Battery-Operated Waterproof Dual Edge Blades for Easy Cleansing - Black
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
This is a flexible device that works great at several occasional tasks...
* I use it to trim my beard down if I have let it go too long. It helps my foil razor shave better to pre-trim with this trimmer if I go a week or more without a shave. Then my foil razor will do the rest.
* Nose hair trimmer is quick and effective. Just move it around in your nose and any stragglers are clipped off in a few seconds.
* Ear hair trimming is also quick and effective with the same attachment. Just run it over any emerging ear hair you see and it will disappear quick and easy.
* I also use it occasionally between haircuts to trim the hair on the back of my neck and re-establish a clean hair line until my next haircut.

It is small enough to leave around on your bathroom counter so that it is available if you notice a straggling nose or ear hair that needs to be addressed.

It is very easy to swap out the attachments.

It is easy to clean with the cleaning brush or running the attachment under the water -- I don't let the main unit get wet even though it claims some level of water resistance.

Operation is super easy with just a simple on/off switch -- no complicated controls or speeds to fuss over.

Charging is easy and the charge lasts plenty long -- wish they provided a USB wall charger so I didn't have to provide my own USB power source.

AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.product_description: Everyday by Formthotics Casual Orthotics - Dynamically Forms To Your Foot, Customizable Support For All Arch And Feet Types, Reduces Injuries, Improves Stability And Comfort, Insoles For Men And Women
AHNIWV6SX76RSYNUAUSFLYFEWQRA.review_text:
I use these as normal replacement insoles, not to address any particular foot issues. I found that they do conform somewhat to your foot as advertised -- it can take a couple of weeks. Overall, I like them and consider them a nice upgrade from my OEM insoles (that were getting a bit worn).

A few things to note:
* You need to allow a couple of weeks for these to conform to your foot
* I found these to be thinner than other insoles I have tried, especially in the toe area where they are quite thin. They are similar in thickness to factory OEM insoles that come in your shoes.
* They don't have the very aggressive arch support that you get with many insoles on the market, so if you are looking for extra arch support or any other "corrective" features, these may not be the best choice
* They seem a bit pricey (currently shown for ~$70) for replacement insoles. They do have the nice feature of adapting somewhat to your feet, but I am not sure I would pay that much extra for this feature.

SUMMARY:
A nice set of replacement insoles that adapt to your feet, but a bit on the pricey side -- perhaps the price will come down a bit.

AHBX5DTXUDNFMGEXKVOS6CD2NLIQ.review_text: I have this installed on my phone, nice to flip on and watch some news at lunch.  I have found, I guess for copyright reasons, if I am 1/10 of a mile from home, at one of my jobs, on a different router, I can not get channels I get at home.  Don't know why, but works well on my phone.  But I purchased a Fire TV this year, thought since I have the app, this will be perfect, can turn in one of those crazy expensive cable boxes.  No such luck.  Works well for 20 minutes, 40 minutes, sometimes up to an hour, then freezes.  Particularly bad if you are watching something you "recorded." Picture freezes, sound keeps going, so suddenly the show has restarted, and picture is still frozen on a commercial.  Pause, exit out of app, re-open app, find show you were watching, restart it.  Only takes 20-30 seconds once you have gotten some practice, but just annoying enough we hooked up our Xfinity box to Fire TV and do not use it.  But does work well, and does not freeze on phone, just channel choice is limited outside if home.

AHBX5DTXUDNFMGEXKVOS6CD2NLIQ.product_description: Borrow eBooks, audiobooks, and streaming video from your library using OverDrive on your Android device. More than 30,000 libraries worldwide offer titles from OverDrive, so download the app and find your next book today!Available 24/7, now the library comes to you.No more late fees. Titles are automatically returned.Place holds, create wish lists, and return titles easily.Sync libraries, bookmarks, and recent positions across mobile devices. A valid account with a participating library, school, or other institution is required.Each library builds its own collection of titles. Contact them directly to let them know that you’re using OverDrive and to share any suggestions for books you would like to see them carry.App requires media player to play audiobook samples.
AHBX5DTXUDNFMGEXKVOS6CD2NLIQ.review_text: Awesome.  I drive a little over an hour to work (thankfully only 1-3 days a week), and recently borrowed a book on CD from the library.  I knew they had them, just had never gotten one before.  Spent the next 2 weeks listening to it.  But switching CD's while driving, not leaving it in the car when it is hot, and then sitting in the driveway listening to the last 10 minutes of one CD was not the best.  I found OverDrive.  You log in, and it connects you to your library (I was connected to the New Hampshire State Library).  From there you can borrow ebooks and audio books. You click on the one  you want, and it sends it to you electronically if available, or you can reserve it if all copies are "out."  If gives me a choice of 7 or 14 days, and if you finish you can return it with a click of a button, so I do 14 days.  Up to 3 books can be out at once.  I have my Kindle hooked up via bluetooth to my car, a bigger speaker at home, and an ear piece, so audio-books I can listen to in the car or home (also can listen through just the Kindle speakers if I wanted).  I have plenty of ebooks I need to read, so have been using this for audio books I want to listen to.  I am hooked.

AHBX5DTXUDNFMGEXKVOS6CD2NLIQ.review_text: After much prodding, I finally got this for my sons (age 7 and 12).  It is easier enough for both of them, but not too easy for either.  I have also played, and once you get the hang of it, it is a fun game.  You get "Cupcakes" by resting that you use to race, and then get coins to upgrade your cart.  You also get gems, which you can use to speed up, or use your "special" power more than once per race.  You get to race about 5 times per cart before you are out of cupcakes, and you can quickly get 2 carts (so about 10 races), then either have to wait about 15 minutes, or my sons found they could race against each other without using cupcakes.  Has nothing to do with Angry Birds, except the characters are angry birds, but cart racing fun!

AHBX5DTXUDNFMGEXKVOS6CD2NLIQ.product_description: WOLF Work Boot | Water Resiliant Genuine Mexican Cowhide Leather | Oil Resiliant | Insulated | Non-Slip Rubber Sole | Roper Boot | Dual Pullers | Composite Toe | Construction | Industrial PPE
AHBX5DTXUDNFMGEXKVOS6CD2NLIQ.review_text:
I have a dog who likes to go outside in all sorts of weather, at all times of the day and night. He is on a leash, can not really trust him to go, do his business, and come back. So we go with him, in all sorts of weather, at all time of the day and night. I was looking for something I could slip on to bring him out, waterproof, good traction. And I am surprised at the quality of these, think they will have more use than just walking the dog out.

First of all, they look good. They are like lower cowboy boots (although I only had cowboy boots as a child, so perhaps for adults they don't normally go up to the knees). These 11-12 inches high (11 inches on the side, 12 on the front and back), so lower to mid calf high. Tall enough to keep snow out, except in really deep snow. They are big enough to slip on. They do slip on, however it is helpful to bend over and pull on the sides. Probably someone with a bit more experience could just step into these and have their foot go all the way in.

I have not gotten to check out how water proof they are, but are a rubber product on the sole, and leather on the top, both of which should be waterproof. I will oil them just to make sure and also protect the leather, but assume, as received, they are at minimum mostly waterproof.

The traction looks very good. Many "cowboy" boots I have seen in the past have a smooth sole, these have great traction, which will be good for snow or slippery wet days.

In my opinion they run a bit large. I am in between a 10 and 10.5 on most boots, so went with the 10.5. I probably could have gone for a 10 or even 9.5 on these. Part of that looseness, I am sure, is because you want to be able to slide your feet in and out, but do feel on the loose side walking around with them, even with thicker socks on. They are too loose for me to use for going on longer walks, but for the dog, for walking out to my truck, to get the mail and things like that will be fine. And certainly if I found myself invited to a country bar for an evening, these would be my boots of choice. Probably not great for dancing in, as they are a bit loose, but would fit right in with others in the bar.

All in all very nice boots. If you are in between sizes, I would suggest you go with the smaller size, or expect them to be loose. Appear very well made and should serve well for many, many years if properly cared for.

AHBX5DTXUDNFMGEXKVOS6CD2NLIQ.product_description: SHORTS THAT SUPPORT. Everything you need wrapped into one, these quick-drying men's shorts are truly your MVP. They feature a supportive compression liner complete with our signature Total Support Pouch™ — a breathable, built-in anti-odor liner that ensures everything stays right where it should. Moisture-wicking DOUBLE DRY™ technology provides a more comfortable experience, so you can stay focused, while stretch fabric and side vents allow easy movement. With a 5" inseam, these men's shorts hit above the knee for functional style. Bring all your essentials with you in the two side pockets, secure zip pocket, and the hidden cell phone liner pocket. The iconic C logo at the hem of these men's athletic shorts delivers recognizable style.
AHBX5DTXUDNFMGEXKVOS6CD2NLIQ.review_text:
It was a little hard to tell from the description, these are lined shorts. So there is a tighter inner liner, with a loose outside shell, similar to what you might expect from old fashioned swim suits (that often have a mesh liner). The special thing about these shorts is that the inner liner has a support pouch.

They are 87% recycled polyester and 13% spandex, so inside layer is stretchy, outside is supposed to be on the looser side, and is. I am not sure exactly how polyester is recycled, but appreciate the company is at least making an attempt to re-use something that might have otherwise wound up in a landfill. The support pouch is on the smaller side, with a lot more support on the sides, than the bottom. There is a little room in there, but would have benefitted from a slightly larger allowance on fabric for the pouch area.

The outside shell has 3 pockets, two front pockets, and a third front pocket with a zipper. A bit unusual, but could see carrying a wallet or phone in my front pockets and putting a key for a locker in the zipped pocket if I were to be swimming in them, or playing a game where I had put my phone and wallet into a locker (like you rent at a pool). Or maybe a good place to keep a hotel key if you are staying in a hotel. In addition to the elastic waist, there is also a cord you can use to tie them up. I am 6 feet, about 180 pounds, 35 or 36 inch waist, and the large fit me perfectly. They are a bit shorter than I might wear for summer activities like walking, but will be great on the beach, at the pool, or when I am engaged in active sports.

Very nice short, a bit more expensive than I might normally pay for shorts, but appear to be very good quality, and also making use of some recycled materials, I do appreciate and am willing to pay more for. They should provide many years of use.
